patch.value: First-party service collision: builtin-calendar and builtin-guide-shell both override the global output_format service

2026-07-04 sandbox audit: with all bundled packages installed, pm extension --doctor warns extension_output_service_override_global:output_format for BOTH builtin-calendar and builtin-guide-shell — two shipped first-party packages contending for the same global output service. Decide the owner (or scope one override) so a full first-party install is warning-free.
builtin-calendar and builtin-guide-shell no longer collide on the output_format service; doctor flags first-party service collisions.

## Context
2026-07-04 sandbox audit: with all bundled packages installed, `pm extension --doctor` warns extension_output_service_override_global:output_format for BOTH builtin-calendar and builtin-guide-shell — two first-party packages contend for the same global output_format service, so a full first-party install is never warning-free and last-activated silently wins.

## Decision needed (then implement)
Pick the owner of the global output_format override (guide-shell is the likelier owner as the interactive surface; calendar probably only needs it for its own command scope) — or scope one override to its owning commands via the action-scoped mechanism from PR#406. Record the choice as a comment here (or an ADR under pm-m2u1 if the service-ownership rule generalizes).

## Definition of done
Fresh sandbox with ALL bundled packages installed shows zero doctor warnings; the ownership/scoping rule is documented in EXTENSIONS.md; regression spec asserts the doctor output stays clean.

value.text: Fresh packed-package evidence (2026-07-10): path-target init --with-packages activated all 11 bundled packages with zero blocking failures, but isolated deep package doctor still reports exactly two extension_output_service_override_global:output_format warnings for builtin-calendar and builtin-guide-shell. Canonical item confirmed; no duplicate created. Temp proof: /tmp/pm-cli-sdk-path-proof-XIsSsh.
